24 Oct 1940 With her trials and training completed, HMS Unique (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN), departed Campbeltown around 1830 A/24 for Portsmouth. She was escorted by HMS Haarlem (T/Lt. L.B. Merrick, RNR) until 1508 A/26 when they parted company near the Lizard.
Before departure HMS Unique had carried out A/S exercises with HMS Tuscarora (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) N.T.B. Holt, RN).
At Portsmouth HMS Unique was to be taken in hand to remedy the problems with the 'singing propellers' of the new U-class submarines. (7)
